Jonas Bobbio Italian monk, author, abbot, active Lombard Italy Merovingian Gaul during seventh century.
best known author Life Columbanus Disciples, most important works hagiography from early medieval period, that charts remarkable journey Irish exile monastic founder, Columbanus 615), through Western Europe, well monastic movement initiated Frankish successors Merovingian kingdoms.
years following Columbanus's death numerous monasteries were built successors their elite patrons Francia that decisively transformed inter-relationship between monasteries secular authorities Early Middle Ages.
Jonas also wrote other, occasional works late fifth sixth centuries: Life John, abbot founder monastery Reome Burgundy, Life Vedast, first bishop Arras contemporary Clovis.
Both works provide perspectives past Gallic monastic tradition, role bishops, Christianization Franks were perceived Jonas's time.
Jonas's hagiography also provides important evidence reception classical late antique texts well works Gregory Great Gregory Tours.This volume presents first complete English translation Jonas Bobbio's saints' Lives with detailed notes scholarly introduction that will value those interested this period.
